IT Accessibility



Back to Scorecard Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override Non-Negotiable?
ITAC-01 Solution Provider Accessibility Contact Name



Not scored


0
ITAC-02 Solution Provider Accessibility Contact Title



Not scored


0
ITAC-03 Solution Provider Accessibility Contact Email



Not scored


0
ITAC-04 Solution Provider Accessibility Contact Phone Number



Not scored


0
ITAC-05 Web Link to Accessibility Statement or VPAT

VPAT can also be added as an attachment
Not scored


0
ITAC-06 Has a VPAT or ACR been created or updated for the solution and version under consideration within the past 12 months?*

If your answer is “I do not know,” select “no.” If the VPAT/ACR is for an older version of the product or has not been updated, its information does not accurately reflect the accessibility of the product under consideration and the response should be "no." Provide a link or attachment to the most recent VPAT/ACR.
Yes
Critical Importance
0
ITAC-07 Will your company agree to meet your stated accessibility standard or WCAG 2.1 AA as part of your contractual agreement for the solution?*



Yes
Critical Importance
0
ITAC-08 Does the solution substantially conform to WCAG 2.1 AA?*

Solutions "substantially conform" if they entirely meet WCAG 2.1 AA or if almost all user and administrator features conform. Documentation about limitations and/or workarounds should be provided where WCAG conformance is not presently achieved. If the solution substantially conforms to a newer standard such as WCAG 2.2 AA, answer "yes."
Yes
Critical Importance
0
ITAC-09 Do you have a documented and implemented process for reporting and tracking accessibility issues?*

Reporting and fixing accessibility issues is critical to a mature process. If the process for this question is merely a "feature request" and tracker, the answer to this question should be "no."
Yes
Critical Importance
0
ITAC-10 Do you have documentation to support the accessibility features of your solution?

If specific configurations, settings, themes, author guides, or instructions are needed to ensure accessibility, are instructions on how to do so provided for administrators and end users?
Yes
Standard Importance
0
ITAC-11 Has a third-party expert conducted an audit of the most recent version of your solution?

Audit results, including VPAT/ACRs, are voluntary reports often generated by the creator of the product. Audits conducted and reports generated by expert third parties give greater confidence to customers.
Yes
Standard Importance
0
ITAC-12 Do you have a documented and implemented process for verifying accessibility conformance?



Yes
Standard Importance
0
ITAC-13 Have you adopted a technical or legal standard of conformance for the solution?

Various federal and state governments in the United States and around the world have mandated accessibility technical requirements that should be considered and may be required when selling solutions to institutions in these jurisdictions.
Yes
Standard Importance
0
ITAC-14 Can you provide a current, detailed accessibility roadmap with delivery timelines?

A detailed accessibility roadmap should reference improvements and progress on known accessibility issues as appropriate but does not necessarily need to list unreleased product features.
Yes
Standard Importance
0
ITAC-15 Do you expect your staff to maintain a current skill set in IT accessibility?

How do you ensure that your professional staff keeps current with digital accessibility laws and best practices? Is your staff able to evaluate and test this product with assistive technologies such as a screen reader or alternative input devices? Examples of staff certification may include IAAP certifications <https://www.accessibilityassociation.org/s/professional-certifications> or §508 Trusted Tester <https://www.dhs.gov/trusted-tester>.
Yes
Standard Importance
0
ITAC-16 Do you have documented processes and procedures for implementing accessibility into your development lifecycle?

Describe where accessibility falls in the development and product lifecycle. Is it at the beginning of your project development or after the product is otherwise complete before launch? Do you incorporate accessibility in your development methods, such as Agile scrums? Does your customer-facing accessibility reporting match your development processes (i.e., Agile methods are best represented using a roadmap and timeline; revised VPAT/ACRs provide a snapshot in time of a given release)?
Yes
Standard Importance
0
ITAC-17 Can all functions of the application or service be performed using only the keyboard?



Yes
Standard Importance
0
ITAC-18 Does your product rely on activating a special "accessibility mode," a "lite version," or using an alternate interface (including “overlay” or AI-based alternates) for accessibility purposes?

Third-party overlays or add-ons are not sufficient for products to conform with accessibility standards. If there is an accessibility mode, does it address a specific accessibility need? Are plans in place to remove the accessible version, and are these distinctions delineated on your roadmap and timeline?
No
Standard Importance
0

Yes

No

No

AI Qualifying Questions



Back to Scorecard Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override Non-Negotiable?
AIQU-01 Does your solution leverage machine learning (ML) or do you plan to do so in the next 12 months?

Answer "yes" even if you do not create your own machine learning solutions, and answer the questions as they apply to your contractual relationship with the third party you utilize.
Not scored


0
AIQU-02 Does your solution leverage a large language model (LLM) or do you plan to do so in the next 12 months?

Answer "yes" even if you do not train your own LLM models, and answer the questions as they apply to your contractual relationship with the third party you utilize.
Not scored


0
General AI Questions



Back to Scorecard Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override Non-Negotiable?
AIGN-01 Does your solution have an AI risk model when developing or implementing your solution's AI model?*

Examples include NIST AI RMF, OWASP Top 10, RAFT, and MITRE ATLAS.
Yes
Critical Importance
0
AIGN-02 Can your solution's AI features be disabled by tenant and/or user?*



Yes
Critical Importance
0
AIGN-03 Have your staff completed responsible AI training?*



Yes
Critical Importance
0
AIGN-04 Please describe the capabilities of your solution's AI features.

Describe capabilities such as content (text, image, audio, speech, video, or code) generation, visual interpretation, and predictive analytics. This encompasses all AI implementations, including third-party AI features. Clarify use cases or limits of the model.
Not scored


0
AIGN-05 Does your solution support business rules to protect sensitive data from being ingested by the AI model?



Yes
Standard Importance
0
AI Policy



Back to Scorecard Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override Non-Negotiable?
AIPL-01 Are your AI developer's policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks conspicuously posted, unambiguous, and implemented effectively?*



Yes
Critical Importance
0
AIPL-02 Have you identified and measured AI risks?*



Yes
Critical Importance
0
AIPL-03 In the event of an incident, can your solution's AI features be disabled in a timely manner?*



Yes
Critical Importance
0
AIPL-04 If disabled because of an incident, can your solution's AI features be re-enabled in a timely manner?*



Yes
Critical Importance
0
AIPL-05 Do you have documented technical and procedural processes to address potential negative impacts of AI as described by the AI Risk Management Framework (RMF)?

Responsible AI development per NIST AI RMF, page 25.
Yes
Minor Importance
0
AI Data Security



Back to Scorecard Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override Non-Negotiable?
AISC-01 If sensitive data is introduced to your solution's AI model, can the data be removed from the AI model by request?*

Please answer based on whether your AI model supports the removal or unlearning of sensitive data, whether it is introduced intentionally or unintentionally. Consider whether data can be traced and deleted from training sets, vector stores, memory, or other components of the AI system. This includes data removal in compliance with privacy regulations and customer requests.
Yes
Critical Importance
0
AISC-02 Is user input data used to influence your solution's AI model?*

Please answer based on whether your solution uses user input data (e.g., prompts, uploads, queries) to fine-tune, train, or otherwise influence the behavior of your AI model. Consider any use of user data for model improvement, personalization, or aggregated learning.
No
Critical Importance
0
AISC-03 Do you provide logging for your solution's AI feature(s) that includes user, date, and action taken?*

Please answer based on whether your AI features generate audit logs that record user identity, timestamp, and actions taken. Include log retention, immutability, access for administrators or auditors, and how logs support compliance and incident response.
Yes
Critical Importance
0
AISC-04 Please describe how you validate user inputs.

Please describe how your solution validates user inputs, including detection of anomalies, malicious inputs, and sensitive data. Indicate where validation occurs and how it supports security and compliance.
Not scored


0
AISC-05 Do you plan for and mitigate supply-chain risk related to your AI features?



Yes
Standard Importance
0
AI Machine Learning



Back to Scorecard Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override Non-Negotiable?
AIML-01 Do you separate ML training data from your ML solution data?*

Please answer based on whether training data is kept separate from production data to protect institutional information. Include how organizational data is segregated, anonymized, or excluded from training, and state whether institutions can opt out of data use for model improvement.
Yes
Critical Importance
0
AIML-02 Do you authenticate and verify your ML model's feedback?*



Yes
Critical Importance
0
AIML-03 Is your ML training data vetted, validated, and verified before training the solution's AI model?



Yes
Standard Importance
0
AIML-04 Is your ML training data monitored and audited?



Yes
Standard Importance
0
AIML-05 Have you limited access to your ML training data to only staff with an explicit business need?



Yes
Minor Importance
0
AIML-06 Have you implemented adversarial training or other model defense mechanisms to protect your ML-related features?



Yes
Minor Importance
0
AIML-07 Do you make your ML model transparent through documentation and log inputs and outputs?



Yes
Minor Importance
0
AIML-08 Do you watermark your ML training data?



Yes
Minor Importance
0

Yes

0

Yes
Critical Importance
0

Yes

0

Yes
Critical Importance
0
AILM-05 Do you limit your solution's LLM resource use per request, per step, and per action?



Yes

Yes

Sheet 10: High-Risk Evaluation
HECVAT Institution Evaluation - High Risk








Version 4.1.5
















Instructions for High-Risk Scorecard












1. The scorecard below reflects those questions marked as "Critical Importance" or those where the "Non-Negotiable" box was checked.












2. Use these condensed, aggregated views to review those questions that pose the highest risk.












3. Changes cannot be made in this sheet. Please make changes in the appropriate "Evaluation" tab.












4. For instructions on how to do a "HECVAT Lite" evaluation, please visit educause.edu/HECVAT.












Solution Provider Name












Solution Provider Contact Name












Solution Provider Contact Title












Solution Provider Contact Email












Solution Name












Solution Description












Date Prepared
12/30/1899










There are cells within this worksheet are auto populated from the HECVAT - Full | Vendor Response worksheet and drop down lists.



























Report Sections Question Count Max Score Score Score %








Non-Negotiable 0 0 0 N/A








Critical Importance/Lite Score 87 1,700 0 0% End Table Data


































Critical Importance Questions (Lite Review Questions)





Non-Negotiable Questions






Code Question Answer Additional Information Analyst Notes

Code Question Answer Additional Information Analyst Notes
1 DOCU-01 Do you have a well-documented business continuity plan (BCP), with a clear owner, that is tested annually?*



1 #NAME?




2 DOCU-02 Do you have a well-documented disaster recovery plan (DRP), with a clear owner, that is tested annually?*




#NAME?




3 ITAC-06 Has a VPAT or ACR been created or updated for the solution and version under consideration within the past 12 months?*




#NAME?




4 ITAC-07 Will your company agree to meet your stated accessibility standard or WCAG 2.1 AA as part of your contractual agreement for the solution?*




#NAME?




5 ITAC-08 Does the solution substantially conform to WCAG 2.1 AA?*




#NAME?




6 ITAC-09 Do you have a documented and implemented process for reporting and tracking accessibility issues?*




#NAME?




7 THRD-02 Do you have contractual language in place with third parties governing access to institutional data?*




#NAME?




8 THRD-01 Do you perform security assessments of third-party companies with which you share data (e.g., hosting providers, cloud services, PaaS, IaaS, SaaS)?*




#NAME?




9 THRD-03 Do the contracts in place with these third parties address liability in the event of a data breach?*




#NAME?




10 THRD-04 Do you have an implemented third-party management strategy?*




#NAME?




11 CONS-01 Will the consultant require access to the institution's network resources?*




#NAME?




12 CONS-02 Has the consultant received training on (sensitive, HIPAA, PCI, etc.) data handling?*




#NAME?




13 CONS-03 Is the data encrypted (at rest) while in the consultant's possession?*




#NAME?




14 CONS-04 Can access be restricted based on source IP address?*




#NAME?




15 APPL-01 Are access controls for institutional accounts based on structured rules, such as role-based access control (RBAC), attribute-based access control (ABAC), or policy-based access control (PBAC)?*




#NAME?




16 APPL-02 Are you using a web application firewall (WAF)?*




#NAME?




17 APPL-03 Are only currently supported operating system(s), software, and libraries leveraged by the system(s)/application(s) that will have access to institution's data?*




#NAME?




18 APPL-04 Does your application require access to location or GPS data?*




#NAME?




19 APPL-05 Does your application provide separation of duties between security administration, system administration, and standard user functions?*




#NAME?




20 APPL-06 Do you subject your code to static code analysis and/or static application security testing prior to release?*




#NAME?




21 APPL-07 Do you have software testing processes (dynamic or static) that are established and followed?*




#NAME?




22 AAAI-01 Does your solution support single sign-on (SSO) protocols for user and administrator authentication?*




#NAME?




23 AAAI-02 For customers not using SSO, does your solution support local authentication protocols for user and administrator authentication?*




#NAME?




24 AAAI-03 For customers not using SSO, can you enforce password/passphrase complexity requirements (provided by the institution)?*




#NAME?




25 AAAI-04 For customers not using SSO, does the system have password complexity or length limitations and/or restrictions?*




#NAME?




26 AAAI-05 For customers not using SSO, do you have documented password/passphrase reset procedures that are currently implemented in the system and/or customer support?*




#NAME?




27 AAAI-06 Does your organization participate in InCommon or another eduGAIN-affiliated trust federation?*




#NAME?




28 AAAI-07 Are there any passwords/passphrases hard-coded into your systems or solutions?*




#NAME?




29 AAAI-08 Are you storing any passwords in plaintext?*




#NAME?




30 AAAI-09 Are audit logs available that include AT LEAST all of the following: login, logout, actions performed, and source IP address?*




#NAME?




31 AAAI-11 Can you provide the institution documentation regarding the retention period for those logs, how logs are protected, and whether they are accessible to the customer (and if so, how)?*




#NAME?




32 CHNG-01 Will the institution be notified of major changes to your environment that could impact the institution's security posture?*




#NAME?




33 CHNG-02 Does the system support client customizations from one release to another?*




#NAME?




34 CHNG-03 Do you have an implemented system configuration management process (e.g., secure "gold" images, etc.)?*




#NAME?




35 DATA-01 Will the institution's data be stored on any devices (database servers, file servers, SAN, NAS, etc.) configured with non-RFC 1918/4193 (i.e., publicly routable) IP addresses?*




#NAME?




36 DATA-02 Is the transport of sensitive data encrypted using security protocols/algorithms (e.g., system-to-client)?*




#NAME?




37 DATA-03 Is the storage of sensitive data encrypted using security protocols/algorithms (e.g., disk encryption, at-rest, files, and within a running database)?*




#NAME?




38 DATA-04 Do all cryptographic modules in use in your solution conform to the Federal Information Processing Standards (FIPS PUB 140-2 or 140-3)?*




#NAME?




39 DATA-05 Will the institution's data be available within the system for a period of time at the completion of this contract?*




#NAME?




40 DATA-06 Are ownership rights to all data, inputs, outputs, and metadata retained even through a provider acquisition or bankruptcy event?*




#NAME?




41 DATA-07 Do backups containing the institution's data ever leave the institution's data zone either physically or via network routing?*




#NAME?




42 DATA-08 Is media used for long-term retention of business data and archival purposes stored in a secure, environmentally protected area?*




#NAME?




43 DCTR-06 Does a physical barrier fully enclose the physical space, preventing unauthorized physical contact with any of your devices?*




#NAME?




44 DCTR-10 Are redundant power strategies tested?*




#NAME?




45 FIDP-01 Are you utilizing a stateful packet inspection (SPI) firewall?*




#NAME?




46 FIDP-02 Do you have a documented policy for firewall change requests?*




#NAME?




47 FIDP-03 Have you implemented an intrusion detection system (network-based)?*




#NAME?




48 FIDP-04 Do you employ host-based intrusion detection?*




#NAME?




49 FIDP-05 Are audit logs available for all changes to the network, firewall, IDS, and IPS systems?*




#NAME?




50 PPPR-01 Do you have a documented patch management process?*




#NAME?




51 PPPR-02 Can your organization comply with institutional policies on privacy and data protection with regard to users of institutional systems, if required?*




#NAME?




52 PPPR-03 Is your company subject to the institution's geographic region's laws and regulations?*




#NAME?




53 VULN-01 Are your systems and applications scanned with an authenticated user account for vulnerabilities (that are remediated) prior to new releases?*




#NAME?




54 VULN-02 Will you provide results of application and system vulnerability scans to the institution?*




#NAME?




55 VULN-03 Will you allow the institution to perform its own vulnerability testing and/or scanning of your systems and/or application, provided that testing is performed at a mutually agreed upon time and date?*




#NAME?




56 HIPA-01 Do your workforce members receive regular training related to the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) Privacy and Security Rules and the HITECH Act?*




#NAME?




57 HIPA-02 Have you identified areas of risk?*




#NAME?




58 HIPA-03 Have the relevant policies/plans been tested?*




#NAME?




59 HIPA-04 Have you entered into a Business Associate Agreements with all subcontractors who may have access to protected health information (PHI)?*




#NAME?




60 PCID-01 Do you have a current, executed within the past year, Attestation of Compliance (AoC) or Report on Compliance (RoC)?*




#NAME?




61 PCID-02 Is the application listed as an approved Payment Application Data Security Standard (PA-DSS) application?*




#NAME?




62 PCID-03 Does the system or solutions use a third party to collect, store, process, or transmit cardholder (payment/credit/debt card) data?*




#NAME?




63 PCOM-01 Have you had a personal data breach in the past three years that involved reporting to a governmental agency, notice to individuals (including voluntary notice), or notice to another organization or institution?*




#NAME?




64 PTHP-01 Do you have contractual agreements with third parties that require them to maintain standards and to comply with all regulatory requirements?*




#NAME?




65 PDAT-01 Do you collect, process, or store demographic information?*




#NAME?




66 PDAT-02 Do you capture or create genetic, biometric, or behaviometric information (e.g., facial recognition or fingerprints)?*




#NAME?




67 PDAT-03 Do you combine institutional data (including "de-identified," "anonymized," or otherwise masked data) with personal data from any other sources?*




#NAME?




68 PRPO-06 Do you have a privacy awareness/training program?*




#NAME?




69 PRPO-12 Do you share any institutional data with law enforcement without a valid warrant or subpoena?*




#NAME?




70 DPAI-02 Is any institutional data retained in AI processing?*




#NAME?




71 DPAI-03 Do you have agreements in place with third parties or subprocessors regarding the protection of customer data and use of AI?*




#NAME?

#NAME?




73 AIGN-02 Can your solution's AI features be disabled by tenant and/or user?*




#NAME?

End Table Data
End of worksheet End of worksheet











Sheet 11: Privacy Analyst Evaluation
HECVAT Institution Evaluation - Privacy






Version 4.1.5














Instructions for Analysts










1. Upon initial review, you can check the "Non-Negotiable" box by any question to compile a report of questions that may prohibit a full review.










2. When evaluating an answer, a default importance level has been set. You can use the "Importance Override" dropdown to override the default and adjust the value of the question.










3. For questions that are qualitative or for which you disagree with the preferred response, make a selection in the "Compliant Override" dropdown to adjust the question's impact on the score.










4. Each worksheet shows a report for that section. See the "Analyst Report" sheet for a full report of all sections.










5. If you are evaluating a question that appears in an earlier section, the Importance and Compliant Override cannot be changed but additional notes can be added.










For full instructions, please visit EDUCAUSE.edu/HECVAT










Solution Provider Name










Solution Provider Contact Name










Solution Provider Contact Title










Solution Provider Contact Email










Solution Name










Solution Description










Date Prepared
12/30/1899






























Cells A20 to A40 have intentionally been left blank. Report Sections Include in Score? Max Score Score Score % Jump to





General Privacy


N/A Jump to General Privacy


PRGN

Privacy-Specific Company Details TRUE 30 0 0% Jump to Privacy-Specific Company Details


PCOM

Privacy-Specific Documentation TRUE 10 0 0% Jump to Privacy-Specific Documentation


PDOC

Privacy of Third Parties TRUE 25 0 0% Jump to Privacy of Third Parties


PTHP

Privacy Change Management TRUE 15 0 0% Jump to Privacy Change Management


PCHG

Privacy of Sensitive Data TRUE 80 0 0% Jump to Privacy of Sensitive Data


PDAT

Privacy Policies and Procedures TRUE 100 0 0% Jump to Privacy Policies and Procedures


PRPO

International Privacy TRUE 50 0 0% Jump to International Privacy


INTL

Data Privacy TRUE 150 0 0% Jump to Data Privacy


DRPV

Privacy and AI TRUE 80 0 0% Jump to Privacy and AI


DPAI

Privacy Score
540 0 0%


End Table Data




































HECVAT Analyst Report - Privacy










Institution Assessment










Instructions for Analysts










1. Upon initial review, you can check the "Non-Negotiable" box by any question to compile a report of questions that may prohibit a full review.










2. When evaluating an answer, a default importance level has been set. You can use the "Importance Override" dropdown to override the default and adjust the value of the question.










3. For questions that are qualitative or for which you disagree with the preferred response, make a selection in the "Compliant Override" dropdown to adjust the question's impact on the score.










4. Each worksheet shows a report for that section. See the "Analyst Report" sheet for a full report of all sections.










5. If you are evaluating a question that appears in an earlier section, the Importance and Compliant Override cannot be changed but additional notes can be added.










For full instructions, please visit EDUCAUSE.edu/HECVAT















Analyst Notes Analysts: Use columns G and I to override the response compliance and importance level.




ID Question Vendor Answer Additional Information Guidance (Will reflect across applicable tabs) Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override Non-Negotiable?
General Privacy



Back to Scorecard Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override

PRGN-01 Does your solution process FERPA-related data?

FERPA-related data includes any data maintained by (or on behalf of) the institution that is directly related to an identifiable student.
Not scored


0
PRGN-02 Does your solution process GDPR-related or PIPL-related data?

GDPR data includes any data related to an identified or identifiable natural person physically located in the European Economic Area (EEA).
PIPL-related data includes any personal data related to an identified or identifiable person located in the People's Republic of China (PRC)
Not scored


0
PRGN-03 Does your solution process personal data regulated by state law(s) (e.g., CCPA)?



Not scored


0
PRGN-04 Does your solution process user-provided data that may contain regulated information?



Not scored


0
PRGN-05 Web Link to Product/Service Privacy Notice

If multiple notices are implicated, provide all that apply. If any other documents are incorporated by reference, provide them as well.
Not scored


0
Privacy-Specific Company Details



Back to Scorecard Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override

PCOM-01 Have you had a personal data breach in the past three years that involved reporting to a governmental agency, notice to individuals (including voluntary notice), or notice to another organization or institution?*



No
Critical Importance
0
PCOM-02 Use this area to share information about your privacy practices that will assist those who are assessing your company data privacy program.*

Share any additional details that would help data privacy analysts assess your solution.
Not scored


0
PCOM-03 Have you had any violations of your internal privacy policies or violations of applicable privacy law in the past 36 months?



No
Minor Importance
0
PCOM-04 Do you have a dedicated data privacy staff or office?

This can include another office, such as information security, dedicated to privacy protection.
Yes
Minor Importance
0
Privacy-Specific Documentation



Back to Scorecard Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override

PDOC-01 If you have completed a SOC 2 audit, does it include the Privacy Trust Service Principle?

SOC 2 Type II audits can be conducted for any or all of five trust principles (confidentiality, integrity, availability, security, and privacy). Answer "yes" if your audit included the privacy principle.
Not scored


0
PDOC-02 Do you conform with a specific industry-standard privacy framework (e.g., NIST Privacy Framework, GDPR, ISO 27701)?

Standard privacy frameworks help organizations enhance data protection, mitigate privacy risks, and demonstrate compliance with appropriate industry and regulatory standards. This is particularly important when providing services in different jurisdictions.
Not scored


0
PDOC-03 Does your employee onboarding and offboarding policy include training of employees on information security and data privacy?



Yes
Standard Importance
0
Privacy of Third Parties



Back to Scorecard Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override

PTHP-01 Do you have contractual agreements with third parties that require them to maintain standards and to comply with all regulatory requirements?*

Inclusion of language in contractual agreements ensures third parties are aware of and have agreed to their obligations to maintain standards and comply with all regulatory requirements in regards to protection of personal data they handle on behalf of your organization.
Yes
Critical Importance
0
PTHP-02 Do you perform privacy impact assessments of third parties that collect, process, or have access to personal data to ensure they meet industry and regulatory standards and to mitigate harmful, unethical, or discriminatory impacts on data subjects?

Privacy impact assessments ensure that third-party collection, processing, or access to personal data aligns with and supports your organization's own efforts and commitments to clients. This is particularly important when a specific third party operates from or is subject to a jurisdiction different from that of your organization.
Yes
Minor Importance
0
Privacy Change Management



Back to Scorecard Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override

PCHG-01 Does your change management process include privacy review and approval?

The change management process minimizes disruption and maximizes benefits and should contain a privacy review process.
Yes
Standard Importance
0
PCHG-02 Do you have policy and procedure, currently implemented, guiding how privacy risks are mitigated until they can be resolved?

Policy and procedure should include specific steps to take in the process of mitigating privacy risks.
Yes
Minor Importance
0
Privacy of Sensitive Data



Back to Scorecard Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override

PDAT-01 Do you collect, process, or store demographic information?*

Demographic information is generally defined as the statistical characteristics of a population used to study and understand certain aspects of that population. It can include characteristics such as age, gender, ethnicity, education, religion, geolocation, and occupation. If the information being collected, processed, or stored falls under a particular regulation (or law), check that regulation for a specific definition of demographic information.
No
Critical Importance
0
PDAT-02 Do you capture or create genetic, biometric, or behaviometric information (e.g., facial recognition or fingerprints)?*

Genetic information would include information about genetic tests, genetic tests of family members, actual manifestations of diseases, and family medical records. Biometric information includes elements such as facial recognition, fingerprints, and voice recognition.
Behaviometric information is behavioral information collected and analyzed in order to understand human behavior. The exact elements collected may depend on the requirements of an applicable regulation or law.
No
Critical Importance
0
PDAT-03 Do you combine institutional data (including "de-identified," "anonymized," or otherwise masked data) with personal data from any other sources?*

Institutional data is created, collected, maintained, transmitted, or stored by or for a college or university to conduct operations. Many institutions have their own specific definitions. Institutional data would include data such as financial information, student education records, faculty/staff/alumni data, research data, and data collected for government reporting purposes.
No
Critical Importance
0
PDAT-04 Is institutional data coming into or going out of the United States at any point during collection, processing, storage, or archiving?

Given the vast number of privacy regulations and laws throughout the world, it is important to know when, where, why, and how institutional data is being shared outside the United States. This information is necessary to ensure compliance and to protect the institutional data.
No
Minor Importance
0
PDAT-05 Do you capture device information (e.g., IP address, MAC address)?

Device information can be captured for a variety of reasons, from analytics to marketing to network management and security. It is important to know the details in order to be clear on the privacy implications.
No
Minor Importance
0
PDAT-06 Does any part of this service/project involve a web/app tracking component (e.g., use of web-tracking pixels, cookies)?

Web tracking can be used to identify users via their IP address, login information, browser information, etc.
No
Minor Importance
0
PDAT-07 Does your staff (or a third party) have access to institutional data (e.g., financial, PHI, or other sensitive information) through any means?

Accessing institutional data may be necessary for legitimate business purposes.
No
Minor Importance
0
PDAT-08 Will you handle personal data in a manner compliant with all relevant laws, regulations, and applicable institution policies?



Not scored


0
Privacy Policies and Procedures



Back to Scorecard Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override

PRPO-01 Do you have a documented privacy management process?



Yes
Minor Importance
0
PRPO-02 Are privacy principles designed into the product lifecycle (i.e., privacy-by-design)?

The question is assessing your compliance with Privacy by Design (PbD) principles. This concept, embedded in regulations such as GDPR (Article 25) and other global privacy laws, requires that privacy is not an afterthought—it must be part of the design and architecture of systems and processes from the outset.
Yes
Minor Importance
0
PRPO-03 Will you comply with applicable breach notification laws?



Yes
Standard Importance
0
PRPO-04 Will you comply with the institution's policies regarding user privacy and data protection?

These policies may include specific user consent practices, data classification standards, and handling of sensitive information.
Yes
Minor Importance
0
PRPO-05 Is your company subject to the laws and regulations of the institution's geographic region?

Indicates whether your organization is legally bound by state, federal, or local laws where the institution operates.
Yes
Minor Importance
0
PRPO-06 Do you have a privacy awareness/training program?*

Privacy awareness/training refers to the ongoing education provided to individuals who handle sensitive data to ensure they understand privacy obligations, data protection principles, and regulatory requirements (e.g., FERPA, HIPAA, GDPR).
Yes
Critical Importance
0
PRPO-07 Is privacy awareness training mandatory for all employees?



Yes
Minor Importance
0
PRPO-08 Is AI privacy and ethics awareness/training required for all employees who work with AI?



Yes
Minor Importance
0
PRPO-09 Do you have any decision-making processes that are completely automated (i.e., there is no human involvement)?

Examples of such automated decisions could include automatically denying or approving user access requests, flagging or blocking transactions based on risk scores, or AI-driven decisions that affect user outcomes (e.g., eligibility, grading, pricing).
No
Minor Importance
0
PRPO-10 Do you have a documented process for managing automated processing, including validations, monitoring, and data subject requests?



Yes
Minor Importance
0
PRPO-11 Do you have a documented policy for sharing information with law enforcement?



Yes
Minor Importance
0
PRPO-12 Do you share any institutional data with law enforcement without a valid warrant or subpoena?*



No
Critical Importance
0
PRPO-13 Does your incident response team include a privacy analyst/officer?

Provide an overview of your incident response team membership and its charge, highlighting the privacy analyst/officer.
Yes
Minor Importance
0
International Privacy



Back to Scorecard Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override

INTL-01 Will data be collected from or processed in or stored in the European Economic Area (EEA)?

See GDPR Chapter 1, Art. 4, for definitions.
No
Standard Importance
0
INTL-02 Do you have a data protection officer (DPO)?

See GDPR Chapter 4, Section 4, for DPO information.
Yes
Standard Importance
0
INTL-03 Will you sign appropriate GDPR Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) with the institution?

See GDPR Chapter 5, Art. 46, for SCC information.
Yes
Standard Importance
0
INTL-04 Will data be collected from or processed in or stored in China?

See PIPL Chapter 1 for definitions.
No
Standard Importance
0
INTL-05 Do you comply with PIPL security, privacy, and data localization requirements?

See PIPL Chapter 5 for requirements.
Yes
Standard Importance
0
Data Privacy



Back to Scorecard Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override

DRPV-01 Have you performed a Data Privacy Impact Assessment for the solution/project?



Yes
Standard Importance
0
DRPV-02 Do you provide an end-user privacy notice about privacy policies and procedures that identify the purpose(s) for which personal information is collected, used, retained, and disclosed?



Yes
Standard Importance
0
DRPV-03 Do you describe the choices available to the individual and obtain implicit or explicit consent with respect to the collection, use, and disclosure of personal information?



Yes
Standard Importance
0
DRPV-04 Do you collect personal information only for the purpose(s) identified in the agreement with an institution or, if there is none, the purpose(s) identified in the privacy notice?

This includes quality assurance, marketing and advertising, etc.
Yes
Standard Importance
0
DRPV-05 Do you have a documented list of personal data your service maintains?



Yes
Standard Importance
0
DRPV-06 Do you retain personal information for only as long as necessary to fulfill the stated purpose(s) or as required by law or regulation and thereafter appropriately dispose of such information?



Yes
Standard Importance
0
DRPV-07 Do you provide individuals with access to their personal information for review and update (i.e., data subject rights)?

Such processes would include descriptions of request processes individuals can follow to review their information and written processes a data subject may use to ask for changes or corrections to data held about them.
Yes
Standard Importance
0
DRPV-08 Do you disclose personal information to third parties only for the purpose(s) identified in the privacy notice or with the implicit or explicit consent of the individual?



Yes
Standard Importance
0
DRPV-09 Do you protect personal information against unauthorized access (both physical and logical)?



Yes
Standard Importance
0
DRPV-10 Do you maintain accurate, complete, and relevant personal information for the purposes identified in the privacy notice?



Yes
Standard Importance
0
DRPV-11 Do you have procedures to address privacy-related noncompliance complaints and disputes?



Yes
Standard Importance
0
DRPV-12 Do you "anonymize," "de-identify," or otherwise mask personal data?



Yes
Standard Importance
0
DRPV-13 Do you or your subprocessors use or disclose "anonymized," "de-identified," or otherwise masked data for any purpose other than those identified in the agreement with an institution (e.g., sharing with ad networks or data brokers, marketing, creation of profiles, analytics unrelated to services provided to institution)?



No
Standard Importance
0
DRPV-14 Do you certify stop-processing requests, including any data that is processed by a third party on your behalf?

Provide evidence of existing processes or policies. The internal privacy policy should explain your organization's policies and practices regarding the collection of personal information and other data about individuals.
Yes
Standard Importance
0
DRPV-15 Do you have a process to review code for ethical considerations?



Yes
Standard Importance
0
Privacy and AI



Back to Scorecard Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override

DPAI-01 Does your service use AI for the processing of institutional data?



No
Standard Importance
0
DPAI-02 Is any institutional data retained in AI processing?*



No
Critical Importance
0
DPAI-03 Do you have agreements in place with third parties or subprocessors regarding the protection of customer data and use of AI?*



Yes
Critical Importance
0
DPAI-04 Will institutional data be processed through a third party or subprocessor that also uses AI?



No
Standard Importance
0
DPAI-05 Is AI processing limited to fully licensed commercial enterprise AI services?



Yes
Minor Importance
0
DPAI-06 Will institutional data be used or processed by any shared AI services?

Provide detailed response to the type of data needed for the AI service to function appropriately, the sources of the data, and whether any data shared with the AI service comes from data sources outside the institution.
No
Minor Importance
0
DPAI-07 Do you have safeguards in place to protect institutional data and data privacy from unintended AI queries or processing?



Yes
Minor Importance
0
DPAI-08 Do you provide choice to the user to opt out of AI use?



Yes
Minor Importance
0
























PRIVACY REFERENCE QUESTIONS -these fields cannot be edited and must be changed on the "Institution Evaluation" tab.










ID Question Vendor Answer Additional Information Guidance Analyst Notes Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override Non-Negotiable?
Company Information



Back to Scorecard





COMP-01 Do you have a dedicated software and system development team(s) (e.g., customer support, implementation, product management, etc.)?*



Yes
Standard Importance


COMP-02 Describe your organization’s business background and ownership structure, including all parent and subsidiary relationships.

Include circumstances that may involve offshoring or multinational agreements.
Not scored




COMP-03 Have you operated without unplanned disruptions to this solution in the past 12 months?



Yes
Minor Importance


COMP-04 Do you have a dedicated information security staff or office?



Yes
Minor Importance


Required Questions



Back to Scorecard Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override

REQU-04 Does your solution have AI features, or are there plans to implement AI features in the next 12 months?



Not scored




REQU-05 Does your solution process protected health information (PHI) or any data covered by the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA)?

Answer "yes" if your solution handles personal health information (PHI), either directly or via a third party.
Not scored




REQU-06 Is the solution designed to process, store, or transmit credit card information?

Answer yes if your solution handles PCI (credit card) information, either directly or via a third party.
Not scored




REQU-08 Does your solution have access to personal or institutional data?

This includes patient data, student data, employment data, human research data, financial data, etc.
Not scored




Documentation



Back to Scorecard Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override

DOCU-01 Do you have a well-documented business continuity plan (BCP), with a clear owner, that is tested annually?*



Yes
Critical Importance


DOCU-03 Have you undergone a SSAE 18/SOC 2 audit?



Yes
Standard Importance


DOCU-04 Do you conform with a specific industry standard security framework (e.g., NIST Cybersecurity Framework, CIS Controls, ISO 27001, etc.)?



Yes
Standard Importance


DOCU-05 Can you provide overall system and/or application architecture diagrams, including a full description of the data flow for all components of the system?



Yes
Standard Importance


DOCU-06 Does your organization have a data privacy policy?



Yes
Standard Importance


DOCU-07 Do you have a documented, and currently implemented, employee onboarding and offboarding policy?



Yes
Standard Importance


IT Accessibility



Back to Scorecard Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override

ITAC-05 Web Link to Accessibility Statement or VPAT

VPAT can also be added as an attachment
Not scored




ITAC-07 Will your company agree to meet your stated accessibility standard or WCAG 2.1 AA as part of your contractual agreement for the solution?*



Yes
Critical Importance


Assessment of Third Parties



Back to Scorecard Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override

THRD-01 Do you perform security assessments of third-party companies with which you share data (e.g., hosting providers, cloud services, PaaS, IaaS, SaaS)?*



Yes
Critical Importance


THRD-02 Do you have contractual language in place with third parties governing access to institutional data?*

List each third party and why institutional data is shared with them. Format example: [Third Party Name] - Reason
Yes
Critical Importance


THRD-03 Do the contracts in place with these third parties address liability in the event of a data breach?*



Yes
Critical Importance


THRD-04 Do you have an implemented third-party management strategy?*

Robust answers from the solution provider improve the quality and efficiency of the security assessment process.
Yes
Critical Importance


Consulting Services



Back to Scorecard Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override

CONS-01 Will the consultant require access to the institution's network resources?*



No
Critical Importance


CONS-02 Has the consultant received training on (sensitive, HIPAA, PCI, etc.) data handling?*



Yes
Critical Importance


CONS-03 Is the data encrypted (at rest) while in the consultant's possession?*



Yes
Critical Importance


CONS-04 Can access be restricted based on source IP address?*



Yes
Critical Importance


CONS-05 Will the consulting take place on-premises?



No
Standard Importance


CONS-06 Will the consultant require access to hardware in the institution's data centers?



No
Standard Importance


CONS-07 Will the consultant require an account within the institution's domain (@*.edu)?



No
Standard Importance


CONS-08 Will any data be transferred to the consultant's possession?



No
Standard Importance


CONS-09 Will the consultant need remote access to the institution's network or systems?



No
Standard Importance


Application/Service Security



Back to Scorecard Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override

APPL-01 Are access controls for institutional accounts based on structured rules, such as role-based access control (RBAC), attribute-based access control (ABAC), or policy-based access control (PBAC)?*

This includes end users, administrators, service accounts, etc. PBAC would include various dynamic controls such as conditional access, risk-based access, location-based access, or system activity–based access.
Yes
Critical Importance


APPL-02 Are you using a web application firewall (WAF)?*



Yes
Critical Importance


APPL-08 Are access controls for staff within your organization based on structured rules, such as RBAC, ABAC, or PBAC?

This includes system administrators and third-party personnel with access to the system. PBAC would include various dynamic controls such as conditional access, risk-based access, location-based access, or system activity–based access.
Yes
Standard Importance


Authentication, Authorization, and Account Management



Back to Scorecard Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override

AAAI-01 Does your solution support single sign-on (SSO) protocols for user and administrator authentication?*

Answer "yes" only if user AND administrator authentication is supported. If partially supported, answer "no." Ensure you respond to any guidance in the Additional Information column.
Yes
Critical Importance


AAAI-02 For customers not using SSO, does your solution support local authentication protocols for user and administrator authentication?*



Yes
Critical Importance


AAAI-12 For customers not using SSO, does your application support integration with other authentication and authorization systems?



Yes
Standard Importance


AAAI-13 Do you allow the customer to specify attribute mappings for any needed information beyond a user identifier? (e.g., Reference eduPerson, ePPA/ePPN/ePE)



Yes
Standard Importance


AAAI-17 For customers not using SSO, does your application and/or user frontend/portal support multifactor authentication (e.g., Duo, Google Authenticator, OTP, etc.)?



Yes
Minor Importance


Change Management



Back to Scorecard Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override

CHNG-01 Will the institution be notified of major changes to your environment that could impact the institution's security posture?*



Yes
Critical Importance


CHNG-02 Does the system support client customizations from one release to another?*

Ensure that all relevant details pertaining to CHNG-06 are clearly stated in your response.
Yes
Critical Importance


Data



Back to Scorecard Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override

DATA-03 Is the storage of sensitive data encrypted using security protocols/algorithms (e.g., disk encryption, at-rest, files, and within a running database)?*



Yes
Critical Importance


DATA-04 Do all cryptographic modules in use in your solution conform to the Federal Information Processing Standards (FIPS PUB 140-2 or 140-3)?*



Yes
Critical Importance


DATA-06 Are ownership rights to all data, inputs, outputs, and metadata retained even through a provider acquisition or bankruptcy event?*



Yes
Critical Importance


DATA-07 Do backups containing the institution's data ever leave the institution's data zone either physically or via network routing?*



No
Critical Importance


DATA-08 Is media used for long-term retention of business data and archival purposes stored in a secure, environmentally protected area?*



Yes
Critical Importance


DATA-09 At the completion of this contract, will data be returned to the institution and/or deleted from all your systems and archives?

Please specify if it will be returned, deleted, or both.
Yes
Standard Importance


DATA-10 Can the institution extract a full or partial backup of data?



Yes
Standard Importance


DATA-11 Do current backups include all operating system software, utilities, security software, application software, and data files necessary for recovery?



Yes
Standard Importance


DATA-12 Are you performing off-site backups (i.e., digitally moved off site)?



Yes
Standard Importance


DATA-13 Are physical backups taken off-site (i.e., physically moved off site)?



Yes
Standard Importance


DATA-14 Are data backups encrypted?



Yes
Minor Importance


DATA-15 Do you have a media handling process that is documented and currently implemented that meets established business needs and regulatory requirements, including end-of-life, repurposing, and data-sanitization procedures?



Yes
Standard Importance


DATA-17 Does your staff (or third party) have access to institutional data (e.g., financial, PHI, or other sensitive information) through any means?



No
Standard Importance


DATA-22 Are involatile backup copies made according to predefined schedules and securely stored and protected?

Ensure that response addresses involatile storage and lists retention periods.
Yes
Minor Importance


DATA-23 Do you have a cryptographic key management process (generation, exchange, storage, safeguards, use, vetting, and replacement) that is documented and currently implemented, for all system components (e.g., database, system, web, etc.)?

Summarize your cryptographic key management process.
Yes
Minor Importance


Datacenter



Back to Scorecard Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override

DCTR-01 Select your hosting option.

If you are using an option not listed, or a combination of options, select "Other." Your selection here will determine which questions below are required.
Not scored




DCTR-03 Are you generally able to accommodate storing each institution's data within its geographic region?



Yes
Standard Importance


Firewalls, IDS, IPS, and Networking



Back to Scorecard Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override

FIDP-01 Are you utilizing a stateful packet inspection (SPI) firewall?*



Yes
Critical Importance


FIDP-02 Do you have a documented policy for firewall change requests?*



Yes
Critical Importance


FIDP-03 Have you implemented an intrusion detection system (network-based)?*



Yes
Critical Importance


FIDP-04 Do you employ host-based intrusion detection?*



Yes
Critical Importance


FIDP-05 Are audit logs available for all changes to the network, firewall, IDS, and IPS systems?*



Yes
Critical Importance


FIDP-09 Are you employing any next-generation persistent threat (NGPT) monitoring?



Yes
Standard Importance


Policies, Processes, and Procedures



Back to Scorecard Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override

PPPR-03 Is your company subject to the institution's geographic region's laws and regulations?*

State the country that governs and regulates your company.
Yes
Critical Importance


PPPR-04 Can you accommodate encryption requirements using open standards?



Yes
Standard Importance


PPPR-10 Will you comply with applicable breach notification laws?



Yes
Minor Importance


PPPR-11 Do you have an information security awareness program?



Yes
Minor Importance


PPPR-12 Is security awareness training mandatory for all employees?



Yes
Minor Importance


PPPR-14 Do you have documented, and currently implemented, internal audit processes and procedures?



Yes
Minor Importance


PPPR-15 Does your organization have physical security controls and policies in place?



Yes
Minor Importance


Incident Handling



Back to Scorecard Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override

HFIH-01 Do you have a formal incident response plan?



Yes
Standard Importance


HFIH-02 Do you either have an internal incident response team or retain an external team?



Yes
Minor Importance


HFIH-03 Do you have the capability to respond to incidents on a 24 x 7 x 365 basis?



Yes
Minor Importance


HFIH-04 Do you carry cyber-risk insurance to protect against unforeseen service outages, data that is lost or stolen, and security incidents?



Yes
Minor Importance


Vulnerability Management



Back to Scorecard Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override

VULN-01 Are your systems and applications scanned with an authenticated user account for vulnerabilities (that are remediated) prior to new releases?*



Yes
Critical Importance


VULN-02 Will you provide results of application and system vulnerability scans to the institution?*



Yes
Critical Importance


VULN-04 Have your systems and applications had a third-party security assessment completed in the last year?



Yes
Standard Importance


VULN-06 Are your systems and applications regularly scanned externally for vulnerabilities?



Yes
Minor Importance


HIPAA Compliance



Back to Scorecard Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override

HIPA-01 Do your workforce members receive regular training related to the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) Privacy and Security Rules and the HITECH Act?*

Refer to HIPAA regulations documentation for supplemental guidance in this section.
Yes
Critical Importance


HIPA-02 Have you identified areas of risk?*

Refer to HIPAA regulations documentation for supplemental guidance in this section.
Yes
Critical Importance


HIPA-03 Have the relevant policies/plans been tested?*

Refer to HIPAA regulations documentation for supplemental guidance in this section.
Yes
Critical Importance


HIPA-04 Have you entered into a Business Associate Agreements with all subcontractors who may have access to protected health information (PHI)?*

Refer to HIPAA regulations documentation for supplemental guidance in this section.
Yes
Critical Importance


HIPA-05 Do you monitor or receive information regarding changes in HIPAA regulations?

Refer to HIPAA regulations documentation for supplemental guidance in this section.
Yes
Standard Importance


HIPA-06 Has your organization designated HIPAA Privacy and Security officers as required by the rules?

Refer to HIPAA regulations documentation for supplemental guidance in this section.
Yes
Standard Importance


HIPA-07 Do you comply with the requirements of the Health Information Technology for Economic and Clinical Health Act (HITECH)?

Refer to HIPAA regulations documentation for supplemental guidance in this section.
Yes
Standard Importance


HIPA-08 Have you conducted a risk analysis as required under the HIPAA Security Rule?

Refer to HIPAA regulations documentation for supplemental guidance in this section.
Yes
Standard Importance


HIPA-09 Have you taken actions to mitigate the identified risks?

Refer to HIPAA regulations documentation for supplemental guidance in this section.
Yes
Standard Importance


HIPA-10 Does your application require user and system administrator password changes at a frequency no greater than 90 days?

Refer to HIPAA regulations documentation for supplemental guidance in this section.
Yes
Standard Importance


HIPA-11 Does your application require users to set their own password after an administrator reset or on first use of the account?

Refer to HIPAA regulations documentation for supplemental guidance in this section.
Yes
Standard Importance


HIPA-12 Does your application lock out an account after a number of failed login attempts?

Refer to HIPAA regulations documentation for supplemental guidance in this section.
Yes
Standard Importance


HIPA-13 Does your application automatically lock or log-out an account after a period of inactivity?

Refer to HIPAA regulations documentation for supplemental guidance in this section.
Yes
Standard Importance


HIPA-14 Are passwords visible in plain text, whether when stored or entered, including service level accounts (i.e., database accounts, etc.)?

Refer to HIPAA regulations documentation for supplemental guidance in this section.
No
Standard Importance


HIPA-15 If the application is institution-hosted, can all service level and administrative account passwords be changed by the institution?

Refer to HIPAA regulations documentation for supplemental guidance in this section.
Yes
Standard Importance


HIPA-16 Does your application provide the ability to define user access levels?

Refer to HIPAA regulations documentation for supplemental guidance in this section.
Yes
Standard Importance


HIPA-17 Does your application support varying levels of access to administrative tasks defined individually per user?

Refer to HIPAA regulations documentation for supplemental guidance in this section.
Yes
Standard Importance


HIPA-18 Does your application support varying levels of access to records based on user ID?

Refer to HIPAA regulations documentation for supplemental guidance in this section.
No
Standard Importance


HIPA-19 Is there a limit to the number of groups to which a user can be assigned?

Refer to HIPAA regulations documentation for supplemental guidance in this section.
Yes
Standard Importance


HIPA-20 Do accounts used for solution provider-supplied remote support abide by the same authentication policies and access logging as the rest of the system?

Refer to HIPAA regulations documentation for supplemental guidance in this section.
Yes
Standard Importance


HIPA-21 Does the application log record access including specific user, date/time of access, and originating IP or device?

Refer to HIPAA regulations documentation for supplemental guidance in this section.
Yes
Standard Importance


HIPA-22 Does the application log administrative activity, such as user account access changes and password changes, including specific user, date/time of changes, and originating IP or device?

Refer to HIPAA regulations documentation for supplemental guidance in this section.
Yes
Standard Importance


HIPA-23 Do you retain logs for at least as long as required by HIPAA regulations?

Refer to HIPAA regulations documentation for supplemental guidance in this section.
Yes
Standard Importance


HIPA-24 Can the application logs be archived?

Refer to HIPAA regulations documentation for supplemental guidance in this section.
Yes
Standard Importance


HIPA-25 Can the application logs be saved externally?

Refer to HIPAA regulations documentation for supplemental guidance in this section.
Yes
Standard Importance


HIPA-26 Do you have a disaster recovery plan and emergency mode operation plan?

Refer to HIPAA regulations documentation for supplemental guidance in this section.
Yes
Standard Importance


HIPA-27 Can you provide a HIPAA compliance attestation document?

Refer to HIPAA regulations documentation for supplemental guidance in this section.
Yes
Standard Importance


HIPA-28 Are you willing to enter into a Business Associate Agreement (BAA)?

Refer to HIPAA regulations documentation for supplemental guidance in this section.
Yes
Standard Importance


HIPA-29 Do your data backup and retention policies and practices meet HIPAA requirements?

Refer to HIPAA regulations documentation for supplemental guidance in this section.
Yes
Minor Importance


Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS)



Back to Scorecard Compliant Response Compliant Override Default Importance Importance Override

PCID-01 Do you have a current, executed within the past year, Attestation of Compliance (AoC) or Report on Compliance (RoC)?*

Refer to PCI DSS Security Standards for supplemental guidance in this section
Yes
Critical Importance


PCID-02 Is the application listed as an approved Payment Application Data Security Standard (PA-DSS) application?*

Refer to PCI DSS Security Standards for supplemental guidance in this section
No
Critical Importance


PCID-03 Does the system or solutions use a third party to collect, store, process, or transmit cardholder (payment/credit/debt card) data?*

Refer to PCI DSS Security Standards for supplemental guidance in this section
No
Critical Importance


PCID-04 Do your systems or solutions store, process, or transmit cardholder (payment/credit/debt card) data?

Refer to PCI DSS Security Standards for supplemental guidance in this section
Yes
Standard Importance


PCID-05 Are you compliant with the Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS)?

Refer to PCI DSS Security Standards for supplemental guidance in this section
Yes
Standard Importance


PCID-06 Are you classified as a service provider?

Refer to PCI DSS Security Standards for supplemental guidance in this section
Yes
Standard Importance


PCID-07 Are you on the list of Visa approved service providers?

Refer to PCI DSS Security Standards for supplemental guidance in this section
Yes
Standard Importance


PCID-08 Are you classified as a merchant? If so, what level (1, 2, 3, 4)?

Refer to PCI DSS Security Standards for supplemental guidance in this section
Yes
Standard Importance


PCID-09 Describe the architecture employed by the system to verify and authorize credit card transactions.

Refer to PCI DSS Security Standards for supplemental guidance in this section
Not scored




PCID-10 What payment processors/gateways does the system support?

Refer to PCI DSS Security Standards for supplemental guidance in this section
Not scored




PCID-11 Can the application be installed in a PCI DSS–compliant manner?

Refer to PCI DSS Security Standards for supplemental guidance in this section
Yes
Minor Importance


PCID-12 Include documentation describing the system's abilities to comply with the PCI DSS and any features or capabilities of the system that must be added or changed in order to operate in compliance with the standards.

Refer to PCI DSS Security Standards for supplemental guidance in this section
Not scored




Copyright © 2025 EDUCAUSE










Sheet 12: Analyst Reference
HECVAT Analyst Reference

Copyright © 2025 EDUCAUSE
Connect with your higher education peers by joining the EDUCAUSE HECVAT Users Community Group



You can find full tutorials on the HECVAT at educause.edu/HECVAT



This sheet provides additional context on each questions for analysts at institutions. In most cases, you will find a reason for each question as well as follow-up inquiries that may be helpful.



Use the "find" feature (CTRL+F on a PC or Command+F on a Mac) to search for a question code



General Information
Reason for Question Follow-Up Inquiries/Responses
GNRL-01 Solution Provider Name


GNRL-02 Solution Name


GNRL-03 Solution Description


GNRL-04 Solution Provider Contact Name


GNRL-05 Solution Provider Contact Title


GNRL-06 Solution Provider Contact Email


GNRL-07 Solution Provider Contact Phone Number


GNRL-08 Country of Company Headquarters


GNRL-09 Employee Work Locations (all) Determines where solution provider employees will be physically located. Follow-up inquiries will be institution/implementation specific. End Table Data
Company Information
Reason for Question Follow-Up Inquiries/Responses
COMP-01 Do you have a dedicated software and system development team(s) (e.g., customer support, implementation, product management, etc.)?* Understanding the development team size (and capabilities) of a solution provider has a significant impact on their ability to produce and maintain code, adhering to secure coding best practices. The size of a solution provider will determine their use of dedicated development teams, or lack thereof. Use the knowledge of this response when evaluating other solution provider statements. Follow-up inquiries for solution provider team strategies will be unique to your institution and may depend on the underlying infrastructures needed to support a system for your specific use case.
COMP-02 Describe your organization’s business background and ownership structure, including all parent and subsidiary relationships. This information defines the scale of company (support, resources, skillsets), general information about the organization that may be concerning. Follow-up responses to this one are normally unique to their response. Vague answers here usually result in some footprinting of a solution provider to determine their "reputation."
COMP-03 Have you operated without unplanned disruptions to this solution in the past 12 months? We want transparency from the solution provider, and an honest answer to this question, regardless of the response, is a good step in building trust. If a solution provider says "yes," it is taken at face value. If your organization is capable of conducting reconnaissance, it is encouraged. If a solution provider has experienced a breach, evaluate the circumstances of the incident and what the solution provider has done in response to the breach.
COMP-04 Do you have a dedicated information security staff or office? The size and capabilities of a solution provider's security program have a significant impact on its ability to respond effectively to a security incident. The size of a solution provider will determine their security operation size or lack thereof. Use the knowledge of this response when evaluating other solution provider statements. Vague responses to this question should be investigated further. Solution Providers without dedicated security personnel commonly have no security or security is embedded or dual-homed within operations (administrators). Ask about separation of duties, principle of least privilege, etc. There are many ways to get additional program state information from the solution provider.
COMP-05 Use this area to share information about your environment that will assist those who are assessing your company's data security program. For the 20% that HECVAT may not cover, this gives the solution provider a chance to support their other responses. Beware when this area is populated with sales hype or other irrelevant information. Thorough documentation, supporting evidence, and/or robust responses go a long way in building trust in this assessment process. This is a freebie to help the solution provider state their case. If a solution provider does not add anything here (or it is just sales stuff), we can assume it was filled out by a sales engineer and questions will be evaluated with higher scrutiny. End Table Data
Required Questions
Reason for Question Follow-Up Inquiries/Responses
Required Questions indicate to the solution provider which questions apply to their product or service.



REQU-01 Are you offering a cloud-based product? The answer to this question indicates if the "Product" and "Infrastructure" tabs are required

REQU-02 Does your product or service have an interface? The answer to this question indicates if the "IT Accessibility" questions are required

REQU-03 Are you providing consulting services? The answer to this question indicates if the "Consulting" questions are required

REQU-04 Does your solution have AI features, or are there plans to implement AI features in the next 12 months? The answer to this question indicates if the "AI" questions are required

REQU-05 Does your solution process protected health information (PHI) or any data covered by the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA)? The answer to this question indicates if the "HIPAA" questions are required

REQU-06 Is the solution designed to process, store, or transmit credit card information? The answer to this question indicates if the "PCI DSS" questions are required

REQU-07 Does operating your solution require the institution to operate a physical or virtual appliance in their own environment or to provide inbound firewall exceptions to allow your employees to remotely administer systems in the institution's environment? The answer to this question indicates if the "On-Premises Data Solutions" questions are required
End Table Data
Documentation
Reason for Question Follow-Up Inquiries/Responses
DOCU-01 Do you have a well-documented business continuity plan (BCP), with a clear owner, that is tested annually?* Testing a business continuity plan is an important action that improves the efficiency and accuracy of a solution provider's continuity plans. Vague responses to this question should be met with concern and appropriate follow-up, based on your institutions risk tolerance.

DOCU-02 Do you have a well-documented disaster recovery plan (DRP), with a clear owner, that is tested annually?* Testing a disaster recovery plan is an important action that improves the efficiency and accuracy of a solution provider's recovery plans. Vague responses to this question should be met with concern and appropriate follow-up, based on your institutions risk tolerance.

DOCU-03 Have you undergone a SSAE 18/SOC 2 audit? SSAE 18 and SOC2 audits are standard documentation, relevant to institutions requiring a solution provider to undergo SSAE 18 audits. Follow-up inquiries for SSAE 18 content will be institution/implementation specific.
DOCU-04 Do you conform with a specific industry standard security framework (e.g., NIST Cybersecurity Framework, CIS Controls, ISO 27001, etc.)? The details of the standard are not the focus here; it is the fact that a solution provider builds their environment around a standard and that they continually evaluate and assess their security programs. In an ideal world, a solution provider will conform to an industry framework that is adopted by an institution. When this synergy does not exist, the interpretation of the solution provider's responses must be interpreted in the context of the institution's environment. Follow-up inquires for industry frameworks (and levels of adoption) will be institution/implementation specific.
DOCU-05 Can you provide overall system and/or application architecture diagrams, including a full description of the data flow for all components of the system? Managing and protecting institution data is the reason organizations perform security and risk assessments. Privacy policies outline how solution providers will obtain, use, share, and protect institutional data and as such, should be robust in its language. Beware of vaguely worded privacy policies. Inquire about any privacy language the solution provider may have. It may not be ideal but there may be something available to assess or enough to have your legal counsel or policy/privacy professionals review.
DOCU-06 Does your organization have a data privacy policy? Managing and protecting institutional data is the reason organizations perform security and risk assessments. Privacy policies outline how solution providers will obtain, use, share, and protect institutional data and as such, should be robust in its language. Beware of vaguely worded privacy policies. Inquire about any privacy language the solution provider may have. It may not be ideal but there may be something available to assess or enough to have your legal counsel or policy/privacy professionals review.
DOCU-07 Do you have a documented, and currently implemented, employee onboarding and offboarding policy? Managing and protecting a solution provider's assets through appropriate human resource management is of upmost importance. Knowing how roles and access controls are implemented (directed by policy) within a solution provider's infrastructure during the onboarding and offboarding processes is indicative of how access control is regarded in other areas on the provider (solution provider). Unsatisfactory answers should be met with questions about access control authority, roles and responsibilities (of access grantors), administrative privileges within the solution provider's infrastructure(s), etc. End Table Data
Assessment of Third Parties
Reason for Question Follow-Up Inquiries/Responses
THRD-01 Do you perform security assessments of third-party companies with which you share data (e.g., hosting providers, cloud services, PaaS, IaaS, SaaS)?* In the context of the CIA triad, this question is focused on system integrity, ensuring that system changes are only executed by authorized users. Additionally, it is expected that devices (for administrators, solution provider staff, and affiliates) that are used to access the solution provider's systems are properly managed and secured. Follow up with a robust question set if the solution provider cannot clearly state full control of the integrity of their system(s). Questions about administrator access on end-user devices and other maintenance and patching type questions are appropriate.
THRD-02 Do you have contractual language in place with third parties governing access to institutional data?* The sharing of institutional data to fourth-parties may increase the risk to the institution and thus, we want to know who gets what data, when they get that data, and why they get that data. Follow-up inquiries concerning third-party data sharing will be institution/implementation specific.
THRD-03 Do the contracts in place with these third parties address liability in the event of a data breach?* Knowing the protections and legal agreements in place for third-party data sharing may assist analysts in determining residual risk. Follow-up inquiries concerning legal agreements with third parties will be institution/implementation specific.
THRD-04 Do you have an implemented third-party management strategy?* Every organization needs to actively understand and manage its supply chain. The solution provider's understanding of who their third-party partners are and their ability to manage those relationships effectively and consistently speaks to the amount of risk your institution is taking on by contracting with them. Modern technologies allow for rapid deployment of features and with them come changes to an established code environment. The focus of this question is to verify a solution provider's practice of regression testing their code and verifying that previously nonexistent risks are not introduced into a known, secured environment. If "No," inquire if there are plans to implement these processes. Ask the solution provider to summarize their decision behind not scanning their assets for vulnerabilities. Be sure that the solution provider answers for both systems AND applications. Do not let good practices in one overshadow deficiencies in the other.
THRD-05 Do you have a process and implemented procedures for managing your hardware supply chain (e.g., telecommunications equipment, export licensing, computing devices)? Understanding a solution provider's hardware supply chain can reveal infrastructure risks that may not be apparent by other means. In some cases, the use of trusted components may be favorable. In others, it may initiate the assessment of the solution provider's environment in more detail and/or expand the scope of the institution's assessment. Follow-up inquiries concerning hardware supply chain will be institution/implementation specific.
Change Management
Reason for Question Follow-Up Inquiries/Responses
CHNG-01 Will the institution be notified of major changes to your environment that could impact the institution's security posture?* Notification expectations should be set earlier in the contract/assessment process. Timelines, correspondence medium, and playbook details are all aspects to keep in mind when assessing this response. If the solution provider's response does not cover the details outlined in the reasoning, follow up and get specific responses for each, as needed.
CHNG-02 Does the system support client customizations from one release to another?* The solution provider's solution characteristics and the institution's use case will determine the relevancy of this question. The purpose of this question is to understand the underlying infrastructure and how it is maintained across all customers. In cases where the solution is customized for customer use cases, ensure the solution provider's response covers all aspects of code migration, including backups, data conversions, local resources from the institution, etc., as it relates to code upgrades and/or version adoptions.
CHNG-03 Do you have an implemented system configuration management process (e.g., secure "gold" images, etc.)?* Hardware lifecycles and continuous software updates creates an always-changing landscape in information technology. The focus of this question is the integrity of a solution provider's infrastructure. Mismanagement of system configurations can lead to breakdowns in layers of security.
